tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The results indicate impaired renal function (elevated creatinine, proteinuria, low urine specific gravity), mild hypoglycemia, and metabolic acidosis (low CO₂, high chloride). Urinalysis shows proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), but no evidence of infection. Findings are suggestive of possible diabetic nephropathy or another chronic kidney disease; clinical correlation and further nephrological evaluation are recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
